name: What should I ask a handyman before I book them?
headline: What should I ask a handyman before I book them?

mainEntity.name: What should I ask a handyman before I book them?
mainEntity.text: What should I ask a handyman before I book them?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before scheduling a handyman, it helps to ask **specific questions** so you understand what to expect and avoid surprises. Useful questions include: - “What kinds of jobs do you do most often?” This shows if your project fits their usual work. - “Are you insured, and do you work alone or with helpers?” Insurance protects both of you if something goes wrong. - “Do you charge by the hour or by the job? Is there a minimum charge?” This clarifies the cost structure. - “Who buys the materials?” Decide if you’ll purchase items or if they’ll handle it and add them to the bill. - “When is your next available appointment?” Check if their timeline works for you. - “What happens if you find extra problems once you start?” You want to know how they handle surprises. Pitfalls: - Not asking about cleanup and disposal. Confirm whether they will haul away old fixtures, boxes, or debris. - Forgetting to ask about payment methods and timing. Some prefer payment at the end of each day or job. Write down the answers, and if anything seems unclear, ask them to repeat it in plain language. A good handyman will not mind your questions and will appreciate that you want clear expectations.
